# Test error handling and edge cases

# Test timeout handling with very aggressive timeouts
env MCP_TOOL_TIMEOUT=1s
env MCP_BASH_TIMEOUT=2s

# Test command that should timeout
! bash 'sleep 3'
stderr 'timeout' 'context deadline exceeded'

# Test invalid tool execution
! exec non_existent_command --help
stderr 'not found' 'executable file not found'

# Test invalid JSON handling
cat > malformed.json << EOF
{"invalid": json, "missing": quotes}
EOF

# mcpspy should handle malformed JSON gracefully
exec mcpspy < malformed.json
# Should not crash, may produce warnings

# Test empty input
exec mcpspy < /dev/null
# Should handle empty input gracefully

# Test very large timeout (should not hang the test)
env MCP_TOOL_TIMEOUT=100ms
! bash 'sleep 1'
stderr 'timeout' 'context deadline'

# Test server connection failures
env MCP_TOOL_TIMEOUT=500ms
! mcp-server-start failing-server go run non_existent_server.go
stderr 'not found' 'failed'

# Test rapid command succession under aggressive timeouts
env MCP_TOOL_TIMEOUT=200ms
exec echo "test1"
stdout 'test1'
exec echo "test2"  
stdout 'test2'
exec echo "test3"
stdout 'test3'

# Cleanup
exec rm -f malformed.json
